To cancel implies to decide/ announce that an event will not take place.

Call off means to decide that something will not happen

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. Ayodeji is an ardent supporter of education for the child
solution: Ardent means very passionate and enthusiastic

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The exhibition was an eye opener to all
solution: Exhibition means a public display of woks of art or items of interest

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. As a Journalist, Bola has always had a nose for stories
solution: To have a nose for something means to have an instinct or a natural ability to find/ recognize something

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. Nwankwo was on the verge of signing a two-year contract with the club
solution:
To be on the verge of something means to be very close to it.

To be on the brink also means to be on the edge or border of something.

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The Governor told the chief that he was only on a routine tour
solution: Routine means performed as part of a regular procedure

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The new ruler is big-hearted in his dealing with the people
solution: Big-hearted means kind and generous

question: Choose the option nearest in meaning to the underlined. The essay topic is nebulous
solution: Nebulous means vague or unclear

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The events of last Friday show that there is no love lost between the Principal and the Vice-principal
solution: The term no love lost signifies ill will exclusively. If there is no love lost between two people, they have a strong enmity towards or hate for the other and make no effort to conceal it.

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. If he were here, it could be more fun
solution:
The first clause 'if he were here' reveals an unreal conditional statement that signifies the absence of someone. However the second clause 'it could be more fun'  signifies the presence of little fun.

Option A is the best answer

options.a: He did not show up and so the occasion lacked much fun
options.b: He was being expected to supply more fun
options.c: There was no fun because he was not present
options.d: He was expected but did not show up to liven up the occasion

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. Adawo is an Imp
solution:
An imp is a person with mischievous behaviour.

Option D is the correct answer.

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy
solution:
sycophancy;  using of flattery compliments to gain self-serving favour or advantage from another person.

Option C is correct. 
''The man reasoned that there ought to be a limit to sycophancy'' means that, they should be a limit to how people use flattery compliments on people especially when they stand to gain something in return.

options.a: there can be no favour beyond a reasonable point
options.b: sycophants need not talk all the time
options.c: people should know when not to use flattery
options.d: sycophants should know when to grant people requests

question: Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. The organization is constantly in a state of flux
solution: A state of flux means a state of uncertainty about what should be done usually followed by the establishment of a new direction of action, Flux is used to describe something that constantly changes

options.a: There are periodic changes in the organization
options.b: The organization is facing a difficult period
options.c: The organization is experiencing; good times

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The witness averred that she had seen Dosun at the scene of the crime
solution:
To aver means to establish a fact or assert a truth.

To deny means to disprove of a statement. It is the opposite of ‘aver’.

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The governor's visit is an unprecedented event in the history of the social club
solution:
unprecedented; means something that has never done or known before.
Something that is remarkable.

Option C ''Insignificant'' is the correct option. It means simply means something that is meaningless; too small or unimportant to be worth consideration.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. Our principal took exception to the ignoble role the teacher played in the matter
solution: ignoble implies not honourable in character or purpose

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. He is notorious for his drunkenness
solution:
Notorious means famous or well known for some bad qualities.

Renowned means famous for something for good qualities

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics.

The severity of the harmattan helped me to complete my writing assignment in record time because i had no choice but to lock myself indoors
solution: severity is the state of being severe . the degree of something being bad or undesirable very great; intense. while the opposite of severity is mildness which means not severe; gentle.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. This card entitles you to attend the film show
solution:
‘Entitle’ means to qualify, allow or give someone the right to do something

‘Disqualify’ means to make one ineligible to do something.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. He is loved for his altruism
solution:
Altruism means to have regard for others at the expense of oneself.

Selfishness means to always consider one’s own interest before others.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. The Journalist said he was working freelance
solution:
To work freelance means to work randomly without having a long-term contract with any firm or person.

Dependent means to rely on a particular person or thing. It is the antonym of ‘freelance’ in this context.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. The Flying Eagles put up a plucky defence against their opponents
solution:
Plucky means showing courage in difficult situations.

question: Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word(s) or phrase in italics. We found a shady place for the display
solution: Shady means situated in or full of shade

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The sea wave continue to ____________ the cliff on the west coast constantly
solution: Erode means to gradually wear away or gradually disappear. Erode is used in a context that relates to wind, water or other natural agents.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. I would have been surprised if you ____________ the orange
solution: 'Would have' is used in conditional statements to talk about something that did not happen in the past. In the sequence of tenses, if the main clause contains 'would have', the conditional subordinate clause should be in its past participle.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. By the time the plane reaches Harare, it __________in the air for twelve hours
solution: 'By the time' is an adverb of time that can be used in the present, past and future tense. The question is, however, in the present tense which will be needing a present perfect tense to complete the sentence.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The patient is suffering from ____________
solution: The options contain miss spellings of the word, RHEUMATISM.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. We saw Ifueko ____________ an egg on the wall
solution:
‘Smash’ is the correct answer because the action conveyed is a single action.

In reported statements, writers/ speakers should retain the present tense.

question:
Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap.

Knowledge of figures of speech as well as of idiom and lexical items ____________ in this section
solution:
When either of these words is used; as well as, together with, alongside, no less than e.t.c, the subject of the clause would be the noun or pronoun that comes before the marker of accompaniment. 

Knowledge of figures of speech is a singular subject that should agree with a singular verb

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. The answers to the questions were discussed ____________ the students
solution: 'By' is the best answer to this question. Between can not be considered because the question did not explicitly tell us the number of students involved.

question: Choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap. Now, I am used ____________ bread without butter

eating is present participle. 

used is an adjective that qualiffied the verb eating. therefore it is is right to say ''to eating'' rightly fits into that statement

question: Choose the option that has a different stress pattern from the others.
solution:
1. ability has its stress pattern on the second syllable

2. registration has its stress pattern on the third syllable

3. quadruplicate has its stress pattern on the second syllable

4. revivalist has its stress pattern on the second syllable

 

Registration, therefore, has a different stress pattern from the rest with its stress on the 2nd syllable

question: Choose the option that has a different stress pattern from the others.
solution: All the words are stressed on the first syllable except 'represent' which is stressed on the last syllable.

question:
choose the word(s) or phrase(s) which best fill(s) the gap(s).

For fear of being caught, the thief shouted and cursed the slow bank manager in a language ________much for words.
solution:
"For fear of being caught, the thief shouted and cursed the slow bank manager in a language too much for words." 

The phrase “too much for words” is an idiomatic expression used to describe something so intense or extreme that it's difficult or impossible to describe. In this context, the thief’s language (probably abusive or vulgar) was too extreme to be expressed properly in words.

question: What is the main message of the story about the hunter?
solution:
The story Centres on a hungry hunter who sees a signboard "Food is Ready" and "All you can eat for free, your ancestor have paid for your food" written below it. He eats but is later asks to pay so that his children can eat for free next time too. This means that when parents bestow good counsel to their children, the children should also do the same to their children when they grow up too.

question: What is the Gandhi test?
solution:
The Gandhi test means that if one is doing something and does not mind other people knowing about it, then it's likely that what one is doing is good but, if one is doing something and one will not want other people to know about it then it's most likely what one is doing is bad.

question: The slave who was tortured eventually
solution:
After a shipwreck, a slave who has been ill-treated by the slave masters while he is aboard the ship woke up on an island and found one of his torturers with him. The slave master was badly wounded and unconscious but instead, at taking revenge by killing him or deserting him, he nursed the slave master back to health.

solution:
This statement is a lesson learnt from the story of the slave who nursed his torturer back to health. If someone hurts us, instead of taking revenge and making the cycle of wickedness never end, we should act nicely to such person and such a mean person will, in turn, became a better person, free from evil acts.

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Okibe was rusticated for his [derogatory] remark about the principal
solution:
Derogatory; means showing a critical or disrespectful attitude.

Unsavoury; means unpleasant or morally offensive, the most suitable synonym for derogatory in this regard.

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Some men will continue to cause offences until they are given [a taste of their own medicine]
solution:
The expression "a taste of their own medicine" means to experience the same harmful or negative treatment that one has inflicted on others. In the context of the sentence, it suggests that some men will continue to cause offences until they themselves experience the consequences or negative actions similar to what they have done to others.

Option B, "revenged on," is the nearest in meaning because it conveys the idea that these men will continue their offensive behaviour until they face retaliation or consequences similar to what they have caused to others.

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

Justice is difficult to enforce because people are unwilling to accept any loss of [sovereignty]
solution: Autonomy is the correct answer. Sovereignty means a situation of self-sufficiency and self-governance.

question: His many years of success in legal practice, .............. , didn't come out without challenges
solution: However is used when trying to include a new point, or buttress an old one with a comparison

question:
Choose the word that has the same rhyme 

Thyme
solution: 'Time' sounds approximately the same way when pronounced as 'Thyme'

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket

solution: Upright is correct answer. Virtuous means having and demonstrating high level of moral standards or ethics, which is the same as being upright,

question:
Choose the one nearest in meaning to the word(s) in bracket.

question: A suitable title for the above passage can be:
solution: The passage is expository enough to largely cover mass communication as a career, practice, relative importance and societal impact and effect.

options.b: The mass media and the people
options.d: The good and the bad side of the media

question: The means of mass media can be summarized into:
solution: Mass media means technology that is intended to reach a mass audience. It is the primary means of communication used to reach the vast majority of the general public. The most common platforms for mass media according to the passage are newspapers, magazines, radio, television. These can be summarized into print (Newspaper, magazines) and electronic (Radio and Television)

question: One of the following is NOT true in the passage
solution: Mass communication in the passage was directly likened to having a physical interaction with a person. This makes Option A contradictory with the passage, hence the answer.

options.c: Mass media determine what the public gets to know

solution: From the last paragraph, the ruling class may influence popular thinking and create certain impression and even direct the course of social discussions by simply buying and/or controlling the media.

question: All the following are roles of the media but?
solution:
moralization - means indulgence in moral pronouncements; or the exposition of a particular moral code.

This is not a role of mass media according to the passage

question: Alice, a small fragile-looking Ugandan mother, is dying of AIDS. She lives with her son and daughter in a tin-roofed shack on the .....1... of Kampala.
solution: Outskirts is often used to refer to the outer parts of a town or city.

question: When her husband died of AIDS in 1987, Alice discovered she was HIV positive. Frequent ...2..... of sickness forced her to give up her embroidery job.
solution: Bouts indicate short periods of intense activity of a specified kind.

question: She was so depressed that she just wanted to eat. ....3.... by a neighbour, the mobile care unit from Nsambya hospital went to her home.
solution: A neighbor who saw and observed Alice decided to come to her aid by alerting the mobile care unit

question: They convinced her to start taking proper ..4.......
solution: Medication refers to treatment using drugs or other form medicine to improve a particular condition or illness.

question: After three months of ....5.... treatment, she regained her will to live.
solution: Intensive means thorough and is usually used to describe a situation involving a lot of effort or activity.

question: The AIDS time bomb .....6... on relentlessly.
solution: Strikes as in this context means to occur suddenly and have harmful or damaging effects.

question: The World Health Organization ....7.... that 1.8 million Ugandans are HIV positive, nearly one in eight of the total population.
solution: To estimate is to approximately calculate or form a judgment of the value, number, extent or quantity of something.

question: The Ugandan AIDS Commission set up by President Museveni ..8...... the spread of the disease.
solution: The Ugandan AIDS Commission seeks to check and curb the spread of the disease.

question: However, under-porting and the lack of post-mortem and ...9..... makes it impossible to establish exact figures.
solution: The most suitable and all embracing answer here is facilities.

question: No one ....10.... that it is a disaster far worse than the years of civil war.
solution: Denies is the best answer. It is unarguably true that AIDS in Ugandan is a disaster worse off than the tragedies of their Civil War.

question: He was both a writer and a politician, but he was better ........ a singer
solution: The adverb "as" is the most suitable answer here to govern the noun phrase "a singer".

question: Vacancies in the company will be notified by .....
solution: Apparently, advertisement is the most viable means by which a company publicize vacancy

question: The driver was short of petrol, so he ........ down the road with the engine switched off
solution: Coasted means to move forward in a vehicle without using the engine.

question: He started his career as an ........ teacher
solution: Auxiliary being an adjective to the noun "teacher" indicates providing supplementary or extra help, support or assistance.

question: Jide couldn't have said that. This means that Jide
solution: "Couldn't have" here indicates the impossibility that Jide said it at all i.e it is rather unlikely for Jide to have said it.

options.c: it's not likely jide said it

question: Bolu shall be the M.C. This means that
solution: 'Shall' indicates a future tense, also 'the M.C' indicates a specific qualification or title. It should be noted that the use of article  'a' is used when we mention something for the first time. However, 'the' is used when something is obvious

options.b: no one else will be the M.C
options.c: later in life, Bolu will become an M.C
options.d: Bolu may not be the M.C

question: Bridget denied that she wasn't there. This means that
solution:
'Deny'' is to say that something is not true.

'that she wasn't there' is the accusation.

For Bridget to deny that she wasn't there, she is simply saying it is untrue to say she wasn't there.  Therefore, she was there.

options.a: She confessed that she wasn't there
options.b: She owned up not to be there

question: Had it not rained, it wouldn't have been so bad
solution: The usage of the anomalous verb 'had' alongside with the negatives; 'not' and that in 'wouldn't' means that, it actually rained and that didn't make things good.

options.a: It was so bad because it didn't rain
options.b: it rained and so it wasn't bad
options.c: it wasn't bad because it rained
options.d: it rained and that made it so bad

question: If he had spoken up, he wouldn't have been given. This means that
solution: The interpretation of the statement is that the speaker or person didn't speak up and so he was given.

options.a: he spoke up and was given
options.b: he wasn't given because he spoke up
options.c: he didn't speak up and wasn't given
options.d: he was given because he didn't speak up

question: Just after ...1..... the aircraft gave a shudder and coughed.
solution: Take off; means to leave the ground and begin flight.

question: The pilot, from the .....2... activated the emergency sheet
solution: The Cockpit is the area, usually near the front of an aircraft from which a pilot controls the aircraft.

question: ...to cover the improperly secured left rear ....3....
solution: The hatch is the trap door in the cockpit ceiling which allows the pilot to evacuate the flight control area in case of an emergency.

question: At the same time, he pushed a button on the panel to activate automatic turning, and the aeroplane ....4.... north-east
solution: Veering here means that the aircraft suddenly changes direction.

question: He called the control ....5.... to report the mishap
solution: The Control tower is the air traffic control, and service provider unit which directs aircrafts and advise in non-controlled airspace.

question: and requested clearance for emergency ...6.....
solution: An emergency landing is a prioritized form of landing made by an aircraft in an emergency situation.

question: The plane circled the airport and prepared to return to the airport it had just left. Moments later, the aircraft ...7.....
solution: Taxiing or taxying is a term for the movement of an aircraft on the ground. Seeing as in this case, the aircraft was about to take off again.

question: to stop on a ...8.....
solution: An airport tarmac is the area where the aircrafts are parked, unloaded or loaded, refueled, or boarded.

question: ....9.... in oilskin ran in the light drizzle
solution: The flight engineers are the members of an aircraft's crew responsible for monitoring and operating its complex aircraft systems.

question: and were soon perched on the ...10..... of the plane, from where they began their inspection.
solution: The Wingtip is simply a part of the wing of an aircraft.

question: The employer, not the sales boy and his partner ........ responsible for the loss
solution: Going by the rule of concord, the phrase 'not his sales boy or his partner' is not part of the subject 'the employer'. Therefore, the singular verb should be used.

question: Both questions are alternatives; you answer ........ one ........ the other
solution: Option A is the best answer. Note that 'either' goes with 'or' and the usage of both creates an impression as to alternatives.

question: It is high time we ........ seeing her
solution: 'It is high time' suggests that something should be done and that it is already a bit late.

question: You can have ........ of these two books
solution: 'Either' is used when there is an alternative choice or decision to be made.

question: Either Buntu or you ........ to go
solution:
The rule of concord states an exception, that is when either or neither joins to subjects (one singular and the other plural)  the verb should be chosen by considering the nearer of the two subjects.

This means that if the second subject is plural the verb should be plural

'You' can take a plural form, therefore option B is the safest answer.

question: The prisoner finally ........ that he drank to ........ on the night he committed the murder
solution: To concede is to admit and the excessive drinking of the prisoner caused the murder.

question: Theatre was popular in Africa because__________
solution:
In the passage, paragraph one emphasizes on community participation, peace and progress. 

Option D is the correct answer

options.a: was traditional and simple in nature
options.b: dealt with the political, social, and economic problems of the society
options.d: was communal and reflected the common concerns of the people

question: One of the reasons why theatre is unpopular in modern Africa is that it_________
solution:
In the passage, paragraph 2 tells us drama is often rated as very low on the utility scale.

Option A is the correct answer

options.a: is regarded as not being of much use
options.b: only deals with culture and tradition
options.d: is always funny and looked down upon

question: In the passage, the term "theatre" and "drama" are used _______
solution:
Throughout the passage, theatre and drama are used interchangeably.

Interchange means 'to switch or swap'

question: One of the arguments in the passage is that academic playwrights_______
solution:
In the passage, paragraph 3 and 4 tells us about the use of  European and American theatrical convention as well as the use of foreign languages by African academic playwright in writing. 

Option A is the correct answer.

options.a: express themselves only in foreign languages
options.b: serve as a catalyst in the society's appreciation of theatre
options.c: work against the common man's appreciation of theatre
options.d: make theatre popular in traditional societies

question: By the expression "climb down", the author implies that modern playwrights should _______
solution:
In the passage 'climb down' implies to descend in the use of language to be understandable by the common man'

Option A is the best answer.

options.a: be simplistic in the use of language
options.c: use language that is most appreciated by the people
options.d: wait for the community to catch up with them

question: It is important for university to be independent so that they can________
solution:
In the passage, Paragraph one talks about the need for Universities to maintain a level of independence which enables Universities to thrive for intellectual pursuit. The writer also emphasizes the need for University autonomy. All these points out to the need to expand and function independently.

Option C is the correct answer.

options.c: be left to expand without any hindrances

question: The writer advocates for multiple sources of university funding in order to ______
solution:
In the passage, Paragraph 1 tells us the writer considers it desirable that effort be made to reduce the government contributions in the interest of University autonomy even if the government was in a position to meet all of the university's financial requirements.

Option A is the correct answer.

options.c: ensure that the numerous needs of the universities are met
options.d: maintain acceptable levels of infrastructural development

question: The expression "intricate issues of special concern" means________
solution:
Intricate means 'complex' 

Option C is the appropriate answer

question: The word "diversified" as used in the passage means_________
solution:
In the passage, diversified is used to suggest the need for the sources of university funding to broaden and not be limited to a particular source.

Calculator
solution: Calculator has its stress on its first syllable. For nouns, the stress is usually on the first syllable except if there is a weak vowel ( /ə/ or /ɪ/) on the first syllable, then it moves to the next one. Since calculator ( /ˈkæl.kjə.leɪ.tər/) does not have a weak vowel in the first syllable, the stress is placed there.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

The man, as well as the woman ________ arrested
solution:
In the rule of concord, 'when words such as, as well as, alongside, together with, no less than etc are used, the verb that follows would be the same with the subject of the phrase or clause, that is, the noun or the pronoun that comes before the marker of accompaniment.

'The man' is a singular noun hence a singular verb is needed.

Note that 'arrested' can function as past tense or past participle but in this context, it is a past participle.

However, 'is' is a singular verb used in the present tense, 'has' is a singular used in the perfect tense, 'have' is a singular and plural verb used in the perfect tense and 'been' is used in the perfect tense.

Therefore, option B is the correct answer.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

Many a ____________ to understand me
solution:
In the rule of Concord, 'when many-a is used, the verb and the noun that follows must be singular'

Option B is the correct answer.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

It is you that __________ to write the letter
solution:
The law of proximity states that any subject closest to the verb element controls the verb form.

The sentence has a singular subject 'it' and a plural subject 'you', hence a plural verb is needed because 'you' is closer.

Similarly, 'supposed' is most appropriate because of the relative clause 'that'. This implies that supposed functions as an 'adjective' in this context which means 'intended'

Option D is the correct answer.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

My father _______ on the floor
solution:
'My Father' as the subject is in the third person category, should be followed by the auxiliary verb 'has' 

Think of the verb 'lie' as a word with two different meaning.

The first 'lie' means 'to rest in a horizontal position' The following are its past, present participle or past participle tense respectively, (lay, lying , lain)

The second lie means 'to give false information' The following are its past, present participle or past participle tense respectively, (lied, lying, lied)

Contextually and grammatically option D is the correct answer.

question:
From the words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

 

This class __________students and teachers
solution:
Comprise means 'made up of'. Therefore, to say 'Comprises of/ Comprise of ' is a tautology.

Note that, singular verbs must agree with a singular noun.

The subject of the sentence is 'This Class' which is a singular noun

This makes Option B correct.

question:

solution:

'had' is used to express a past perfect tense as opposed to 'has' and 'have' which is used to express a present perfect tense.

question:

solution:
Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a phrase or sentence. In this case, the word wife is emphasized to correct a mistake that has been made in the question. To find the appropriate question to the response: I bought my WIFE a new dress, you have to find the question that has a word that is different from the emphasized word/ the word in capital letters. The correct question is

A. Did I buy my husband a new dress?

No. I bought my WIFE a new dress.

options.d: Did I buy my wife a black dress?

solution: The correct answer is 'He did not show up and so the occasion lacked much fun'. The other options do not fully capture the essence of the sentence.

Therefore, the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence is 'He takes an unfavourable position concerning our problems'.

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

In Lafayette, before a stranger is hosted or accommodated, permission must be requested and granted by_______
solution: In the novel 'The Life Changer', in Lafayette, before a stranger is hosted or accommodated, permission must be requested and granted by the Hakimi. The Hakimi is a traditional title for a local or tribal leader in some areas of the Middle East and North Africa. This can be seen on page 20 of the novel. It is an important tradition that helps to ensure that no criminal or fugitive from justice is harboured or accommodated in the village.

question: Choose the word that does not have the same vowel sound as the others
solution: The words 'sun', 'shun', and 'son' have the same vowel sound /ʌ/, while 'short' has a different vowel sound /ɔ:/. Therefore, 'short' does not have the same vowel sound as the others.

question: Athens had _________ the Greek-states against Persians.
solution: From the passage, it is clear that Athens had interceded for the other Greek city-states against the Persian domination. This means that Athens had intervened on behalf of the other Greek city-states. Therefore, the correct answer is option 2.

question: Darius took drastic steps to __________ the rebellious Athenians.
solution: In the passage, it is mentioned that 'In anger the king sent an enormous army to defeat Athens. He thought it would take drastic steps to pacify the rebellious part of the empire.' This implies that Darius, the king, took drastic steps to calm the rebellious Athenians. Therefore, the correct answer is option 3.

question: Their participation _________to the Athenians.
solution: In the passage, it is mentioned that 'Emboldened by this participation, Athenians were prepared to die for their city-state.' This suggests that the participation of all citizens in ruling Athens gave them the strength and courage to fight against the Persians. Therefore, the correct answer is 'gave strength'.

question: The people of Delos did not want to __________ the conquest of Greece
solution: In the passage, it is mentioned that 'The frightened city of Delos refused to believe this. Not wanting to abet the conquest of Greece, they fled from their city and did not return until the Persians had left.' This suggests that the people of Delos did not want to encourage or support the conquest of Greece by the Persians. Therefore, the correct answer is 'encourage'.

question: The Athenians were__________ by some soldiers who arrived at Plataea
solution: In the context of the passage, the Athenians were 'strengthened' by some soldiers who arrived at Plataea. This is because the soldiers from Plataea reinforced the Athenian troops, providing them with additional strength and support in their battle against the Persians.

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Salma was looking more stunning on the last day of her exams because_______
solution: Self-explanatory

options.a: her classmates had planned to go to the beach after the exam
options.b: her classmates had organized a mini picnic to wrap up the good work
options.d: her classmates had planned a photo shoot with some lecturers

question:
This question is based on "The Life Changer" novel.

Why was it a double celebration for Ummi and members of her community when she gained admission into the university?
solution: Self-explanatory

options.b: She had given birth to Omar before her admission to the university
options.c: Her husband agreed to marry her before she went for university registration
options.d: she was the first child to leave Lafayette to the university

question: ...1...
solution: This best suits because the senate has to do with the legislative arm and they are saddled with responsibility of law making.

question: ...2...
solution: The context of the passage suggests that the Senate is trying to 'nullify' the executive action of the President. 'Nullify' means to make legally null and void; invalidate. The Senate, being a legislative body, does not have the power to enforce, modify, or order the executive action of the President.

question: ...3...
solution: In political terminology, an "arm" is often used to refer to a branch or division within a legislative body. The National Assembly is a larger legislative body, and the Senate is a component or division within it.

question: ...4...
solution: In the context of the passage, the author is discussing the potential confusion that could arise if another part of the National Assembly were to disagree with the Senate. The House of Representatives is another part of the National Assembly, making it the correct answer. The Judiciary and the Executive Council are separate branches of government and do not fit the context of the sentence.

question: ...5...
solution: In the context of the passage, the Senate is being criticized for overstepping its bounds and acting as both the 'litigant' and the judge of the constitutionality of the President's actions. A 'litigant' is a party involved in a lawsuit, which fits the context better than the other options. 'Defendant' and 'plaintiff' are specific types of litigants, but the passage does not specify which role the Senate is taking on. 'Attorney' does not fit because the Senate is not representing someone in a legal matter, but rather taking on the role of a party in a lawsuit.

question: ...6...
solution: In the context of the passage, the function of the Senate is to 'enact' laws. To 'enact' a law means to make it official or put it into action, which is the role of legislative bodies like the Senate. 'Create' could also work, but 'enact' is more specific to the process of passing laws. 'Compose' is more commonly used in the context of writing or creating something like a piece of music or literature, not laws. 'Annul' means to declare invalid, which is not a function of the Senate mentioned in the passage.

question: ...7...
solution: In the context of the passage, the Senate does not have the authority or 'power' to control the President in the exercise of his powers. The term 'power' refers to the ability or capacity to do something or act in a particular way. The other options 'might', 'dynamism', and 'strength' do not fit into the context of the sentence.

question: ...8...
solution: In the context of the passage, the President is exercising his 'executive' powers. The term 'executive' refers to the branch of government responsible for implementing, administering, and enforcing laws. The other options 'official', 'authoritative', and 'judicial' do not fit into the context of the sentence.

question: ...9...
solution: In the context of the passage, the exercise of the powers of the President can be 'regulated'. This means that the actions of the President can be controlled or governed according to a system or set of rules, which is the role of the National Assembly. The other options do not fit into the context of the passage.

question: ...10...
solution: In the context of the passage, the exercise of the powers of the President can be regulated by 'an Act' of the National Assembly. An Act is a law that has been officially put into effect, which fits into the context of the passage. The other options do not fit into the context of the passage.

question:

solution: The correct phrase to use is 'held up'. 'Held up' is a phrasal verb that means to be delayed or obstructed. So, 'The guest would have arrived earlier but he was held up in traffic' is the correct sentence.

question: He was both a writer and a politician, but he was better ........ a singer
solution: The correct preposition to use after 'better' in this context is 'as'. When comparing roles or functions, 'as' is used. For example, 'He works better as a manager than as a clerk.' Therefore, the sentence should read 'He was both a writer and a politician, but he was better as a singer.'

question:
The primary stress is indicated by writing the syllable on which it occurs in capital letters. From the words lettered A to D choose the one that has the correct stress.
presidency
solution: The correct stress for the word 'presidency' is 'PRE-si-den-cy'. In English, stress means pronouncing one syllable of a multisyllabic word with greater emphasis (stress) than the other syllables in the word. The primary stress in a word is the syllable that is pronounced with the most force. In the word 'presidency', the first syllable 'pre' is pronounced with the most force, hence it has the primary stress.

question: --6--
solution: The correct answer is 'outstanding'. In this context, the coach is asking for the Bright Rangers to be paid their outstanding salaries. 'Outstanding' in this context means 'yet to be paid' or 'owing'. The other options do not fit into the context of the sentence. 'Left over' implies that there is some salary that has not been used or claimed, which is not the case here. 'Owed' could potentially work, but it is less formal and less precise than 'outstanding'. 'Late' implies that the salaries are delayed, which is not necessarily the case - they could be unpaid for other reasons.

question: --7--
solution: The correct answer is 'match bonuses'. In this context, the coach is asking for the Bright Rangers to be paid their outstanding salaries and match bonuses. A 'match bonus' is an extra payment made to players for participating in a particular match, often as a form of incentive. The other options do not fit into the context of the sentence. 'Match payments' and 'match wages' are not standard terms in football, and 'match fees' would typically refer to fees charged to watch a match, not payments made to players.

question: --8--
solution: The correct answer is 'spur'. In this context, the coach wants the Bright Rangers to be paid their salaries to 'spur' them to victory. The word 'spur' means to encourage or stimulate into action, which fits perfectly in this context.

question: --9--
solution: The correct answer is 'from'. In this context, the Bright Rangers have four points 'from' many matches. The preposition 'from' is used to indicate the source or origin of something, in this case, the source of the points is the many matches they have played.

question: --10--
solution: The correct answer is - avoid.

question: --11--

question: --12--

question: --13--
solution: In the context of the sentence, the coach is emphasizing the importance of the players being paid their salaries on time to ensure their motivation for the upcoming match. Therefore, the correct answer is 'on time'.

question: --14--
solution: In the context of the sentence, the coach is saying that getting paid would be a big morale boost for the team. Therefore, the correct word to fill in the blank is 'Booster'.

question: --15--
solution: In the context of the sentence, the coach is saying that the Bright Rangers plan to employ the counter strategy to get a result in Cario. The correct word to fill in the blank is 'Attack'.

productivity

question:

The narrator and her children sat  _______ because the room was stuffy.

They went outside to sit while they waited for their father's return. This can be found on page 6 of the novel where Jamila, one of the narrator's children, says: "Let's go outside and sit under the mango tree in the courtyard".

The quiet one in the text is

question:

What is the status of Habib Lawal in the House of Assembly

question:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

At first, Salma did not like the
solution: Salma, at first, did not like the composition of her roommates. Her roommates: Ngozi, Tomiwa and Ada all came from different parts of the country. She actually liked the "most coveted"  and "famous" Queen Amina Hall but did not take a liking to her roommates. This can be found on page 31 of the novel.

question:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

The use of the expression Notes of Salvation is
solution: Notes of Salvation is a euphemism for the cheat notes that Kola passed to Salma during the examination. It helps to reduce the unpleasantness of what the cheat notes represent.

question:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

The acronym "EMEC" as used in the novel means
solution: The acronym "EMEC" means "Examination Malpractice and Ethics Committee". We can find this on page 59 of the novel. It is in front of this committee that Selma and Kola had to appear for the Committee to decide their punishment for committing examination malpractice.

question:
The following question is based on Khadija Abububakar Jalili's The Life Changer.

How much did Dr Kabir Mohammed collect from Salma in order to cover up her EMAL offence?
solution: Dr Kabir Muhammed collected one hundred thousand Naira from Salma to cover up her EMAL offence. This is after she negotiated it down from two hundred thousand Naira. This can be found on page 61 of the novel.

options.b: one hundred and fifty thousand naira

question:

According to the novel, Bint's Social Studies teacher is
solution: Bint describes her Social Studies teacher as meddlesome as she narrates how she took on her Social Studies teacher who thinks he knows "a little bit about every subject under the sun". This can be found on page 1 of the novel.

question: What option best fills the gap labelled 8

solution: "Bare" describes the exposed or uncovered coal surface. Other options do not fit the description appropriately.

question: What option best fills the gap labelled 10
solution: The "beams" are bars that support the span of the roof between two supports, props or walls.

question: What option best fills the gap labelled 11

solution: A "hole" is what the miners crawl through to get to the coal.

solution: The combination of "digging" and explosives is what is needed to harvest coal from a mine.

question: From the passage, it can be inferred that
solution: The passage explains that the type of weathering depends on climatic conditions: physical or mechanical weathering occurs with temperature and water, while chemical weathering thrives in hot climates.

options.b: Climate determines the type of weathering

question: A suitable title for the passage above is
solution: The passage focuses on the process of weathering, its types (physical/mechanical and chemical), and the factors that influence it, particularly climatic conditions. Therefore, "Weathering" is the most appropriate title.

question: The writer was puzzled because
solution: The passage states, "I noticed big trees with their roots penetrating into rocks and breaking them. I initially wondered and puzzled seeing trees growing from the rock." This clearly indicates that the writer was puzzled by the sight of trees growing from the rocks.

question: Learning in the four walls of a classroom implies
solution: Learning within the "four walls of a classroom" refers to formal education, which is structured and organised and takes place in a school.

question: One can conclude from the passage that
solution: he passage mentions that the writer observed rock particles mixing with the rest of the soil during soil sampling. This implies that rock particles are not exclusive to any specific type of soil; they can be part of soil in general.

options.a: rock particles cannot be found in the soil

options.c: rock particles can only be found in sandy soil

question:
In the following question, select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He was building a castle in the air.
solution: The phrase "building a castle in the air" is an idiom that refers to imagining or planning something fanciful, unrealistic or highly unlikely to happen.

options.b: He was imagining a terrible future
options.c: He was imagining a possible future as a famous architect
options.d: He was imagining a wonderful and rather impossible future

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Jude appears to sometimes enjoy playing the devil's advocate.
solution: The phrase "playing the devil's advocate" is an idiomatic expression that means arguing for a position one may not necessarily believe in to provoke debate or test the strength of an opposing argument.

options.a: Jude seems to enjoy acting as the devil in films
options.b: Jude seems to enjoy playing the game
options.c: Jude seems to enjoy advancing the unpopular side of an argument
options.d: Jude seems to always enjoy appearing differently

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Yerima always holds his own in any situation he finds himself.
solution: The phrase "holds his own in any situation" means that Yerima is capable of maintaining his position, handling himself well, or standing firm regardless of the circumstances. This means that he has confidence and composure.

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Titi was the cynosure of all eyes at the party.
solution: The phrase "cynosure of all eyes" means someone who is the focus of attention or admiration. In this context, it suggests that Titi drew everyone's attention at the party.

options.b: She was the centre of attraction
options.d: She was eager to leave the party

question:
Choose the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

Let this case stand over for the present, the lawyer pleaded.
solution: The phrase "stand over for the present" means to temporarily delay or postpone something to a later time. It is used in legal or formal contexts to request that a matter be deferred.

options.a: The case should be discussed immediately
options.b: The case should be struck out
options.d: The case is pending in court

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge declared the two accused guilty.
solution: The phrase "declared the two accused guilty" means that the judge found the two accused individuals responsible for the crime. The opposite of guilty is innocent.

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

A prosperous lawyer is definitely hardworking.
solution: The word "prosperous" means successful or thriving in terms of wealth or financial stability. The opposite of prosperous is poor.

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

Fake news is rife on social media platforms.
solution: In this context, "fake news" refers to misleading or false information. The opposite of fake news would be authentic news, meaning real or genuine.

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The children's room was messy when we entered.
solution: The word "messy" means disorganized or untidy. The opposite of messy is tidy, which means clean and organized.

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The judge pronounced the suspect guilty.
solution: The word "guilty" means found responsible for a crime. The opposite of guilty is acquitted, which means to be declared not guilty of a crime.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined.

The lecturer's wife has become quite unconscionable.
solution: The word "unconscionable" refers to behaviour that is immoral or unethical and beyond what is reasonable or just. The nearest in meaning to the word unconscionable is unbecoming, which means inappropriate or not in keeping with the standards of behaviour expected in a particular context, though less severe than unconscionable.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He bequeathed his company to his last daughter.
solution: The word "bequeathed" means to leave something (especially property or possessions) to someone in a will. The nearest meaning to bequeathed is left, as in leaving something behind for someone.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She went to the court to seek redress for the wrong done to her.
solution: The word "redress" means seeking justice or compensation for a wrong or injury. In this context, redress means asking for compensation to make up for the harm or injustice done.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The attempt by the robbers to break into the bank was futile.
solution: The word "futile" means something that is pointless or without success. The nearest meaning to futile is fruitless, which also means producing no results or being unsuccessful.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The newly employed teachers gave their work rapt attention.
solution: Paying "rapt" attention means paying full attention or being fully absorbed in something. The nearest meaning to rapt attention is concentrated, which refers to focusing all one's attention on a task or activity.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Sally assisted me in buying my _____ yesterday.
solution: The phrase "very first party dress" is the grammatically correct option because the word "very" is used to emphasise the first party dress, which is the key element in the sentence.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

You know that you ____ it before tomorrow.
solution: The phrase "ought to do" means that there is an expectation or obligation to complete something by tomorrow.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Maryam, the girl in my neighbourhood, treats people as if she _____ better than they are.
solution: The sentence uses a hypothetical situation (Maryam treating people as if she is better than they are), and in such cases, we use the subjunctive mood, which requires the verb "were" instead of "is" or "was" when expressing unreal or hypothetical conditions.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

But for the timely intervention of the police, the thief ______ by the angry mob.
solution: The phrase "But for the timely intervention of the police" indicates a hypothetical situation in the past, which suggests that the thief would have been lynched if the police hadn't intervened. In such a situation, the correct form to use is the past perfect conditional, "would have been lynched."

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My father loves bringing ___ old memories.
solution: The phrase "bringing up old memories" is an idiomatic expression that means to mention or recall past memories.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Women always ______ themselves in beautiful apparel.
solution: The word "adorn" means to decorate or add beauty to oneself or something.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The movie wasn't fantastic, was it? _____
solution: The appropriate answer to this is "No, it wasn't" to show agreement with the speaker. Question tags are used to elicit agreement from a listener and therefore the most appropriate response should be one of agreement.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

My uncle is not sure ____ the strange guests were.
solution: The sentence refers to people, specifically the "strange guests," so the correct word to use is "who", which is used to refer to people.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

Everyone in class wanted the Literature teacher to ______ the point of view of the _______.
solution: "Expatiate" means to elaborate or explain something in detail. "Playwright" is someone who writes plays. Other options have wrong spellings of the words.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

____ of his lateness, Okupe gained from the coaching.
solution: "In spite of" is used to show contrast with something (in this case, Okupe's lateness). The phrase means that despite being late, Okupe still benefited from the coaching.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

The man is not as powerful as he once was, his influence has considerably _______
solution: "Diminished" means reduced in size, strength or importance. It shows that the man's influence has lessened over time.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence.

I can't lend him my books. I ______ know him.
solution: "Scarcely" means "hardly" or "barely," which means that the speaker knows the person only to a very limited extent, making it unlikely for the speaker to lend the books.

question: Honeybees have two characteristics similar to humans.
solution: The passage mentions that honeybees are social insects and live together in hives. Additionally, it highlights that they communicate with each other through a unique dancing movement to share information about food sources. This reflects a social structure and communication, which is a characteristic similar to humans.

options.a: they have both male and female sexes
options.b: they have a caste system and work for a living
options.d: they are social and communicate with one another

question: According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce
solution:
According to the passage, it is not all honeybees that produce eggs. The passage states that only the queens lay eggs:

"The queens' unfertilised eggs develop into drones, whereas fertilised eggs develop into females, which may be either workers or virgin queens."

question: Another word for the word destined according to the passage would be
solution: In the passage, the word "destined" refers to the predetermined fate or purpose of the eggs in the honeybee colony. It mentions that "eggs destined to become queens" are deposited in queen cells, meaning these eggs are predetermined to develop into queens.

question: Apart from being born royal, a queen can lose its royalty by becoming a worker through
solution: According to the passage, the fate of a virgin queen is determined by the diet she is fed. If she is fed a diet consisting solely of royal jelly, she develops into a queen. However, if she is not fed this diet, she will develop into a worker instead.

question: Which of the honeybees does not live long?
solution: According to the passage, drones (the male honeybees) are present only for about eight weeks in early summer. They live for a shorter period compared to the queens and workers, who have longer lifespans.

"The paper stated that the driver of the lorry was helping the police in their investigation" means that the driver is cooperating with the police and providing information to assist their investigation.

When a news report says someone is "helping police with their inquiries" or "helping in their investigation," it is a polite way of saying that the person is being questioned by the police in connection with an incident or crime. It doesn't necessarily mean the person is under arrest, but they are providing information that the police believe is relevant to their investigation.

Due to old age, mother has become taciturn.
solution: The word taciturn means someone who is habitually quiet or reserved in speech. The opposite is garrulous, which describes someone who is excessively talkative.

question:

question:

immunity
solution: For multi-syllabic words that end in '-ty', the stress tends to fall on the ante-penultimate syllable (i.e. the third syllable from the last). Therefore, the stress placement would be iMMUnity.

question:
In each of the following questions, choose the most appropriate stress pattern from the options. The stressed syllable is written in capital letters. 

radioactive
solution: Words that end with -ive have their stress on the penultimate syllable (i.e. on the second to the last syllable). Therefore, the stress placement is radioACtive.

question:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

His reign was noted for PEACE.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "Peace" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "peace". The answer is option A because the emphatic stress on PEACE contrasts with wealth in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word PEACE in the response.

options.a: Was his reign noted for wealth?
options.b: Whose house is noted for peace?
options.c: Was his reign examined for peace?
options.d: Whose reign is noted for peace?

question:
In each of the following questions, the word in capital letter has the emphatic stress. Choose the option to which the given words relate. 

Bala ate a slice of BREAD.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particulate word in a sentence for clarification or emphasis. The question requires you to select the question that incites the response given. The word "bread" is emphasised in the response, and so you are to look for an option that has a word that is different from "bread". The answer is option D because the emphatic stress on BREAD contrasts with cake in the question, thereby validating and emphasizing the word BREAD in the response.

options.a: Did Bala drink a slice of bread?
options.b: Did Bala eat a loaf of bread?
options.c: Did Olu eat a slice of bread?
options.d: Did Bala eat a slice of cake?

question: The word anguish as used in the passage means
solution: In the context of the passage, anguish refers to extreme suffering or pain, both physical and emotional, as described by the harrowing experiences of the people.

question: The phrase on that fateful day is
solution: The phrase on that fateful day modifies the verb had pursued by providing information about when the action occurred, making it an adverbial phrase.

question: From the passage, it is clear that
solution: This is evident from the passage where it is stated, "Some of them bitten by snakes died because we were not able to carry them to hospital for treatment."

options.a: everyone from the writer's village arrived Cameroon alive
options.b: everyone from the writer's village arrived the mountains alive
options.c: some children were not able to make it to Cameroon
options.d: all children made it to Cameroon alive despite the snakebites

question: What does the writer mean by we knew what we were up against?
solution: The phrase shows the writer's awareness of the dangerous and violent nature of the insurgents pursuing them.

options.a: The height of the mountains they were running to
options.c: The difficulty of life on the mountains
options.d: The distance from the mountains to Cameroon

question: According to the writer, travelling across several labyrinths suggests
solution: The phrase "travelling across several labyrinths" suggests a challenging and confusing journey. It shows the difficulty in finding their way to Cameroon.

options.a: it was easy for them to locate the paths leading to Cameroon
options.b: Cameroon is a straight distance from the mountains
options.c: they arrived Cameroon after two days of crossing several rivers
options.d: they had a complicated and tortuous journey through the bush

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

It is highly improbable that it will snow in June.
solution: The word "improbable" means "unlikely to happen". The other options either exaggerate the sentence or does not convey the information in the sentence at all.

options.a: It is highly unlikely that it will snow in June
options.b: It is possible that it will snow in June
options.c: It will never snow in June
options.d: The snowfall will probably be high in June

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

He played his wife false by inviting his mistress to the house as his cousin.
solution: The phrase "played his wife false" means he acted deceitfully toward her, and the fact that he invited his mistress to the house as his cousin implies he was unfaithful to his wife.

options.a: he brought his cousin to his wife's room
options.b: His cousin and his wife were friends
options.c: He cheated on his wife with his mistress
options.d: He helped his wife with his mistress

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

Driving in Lagos is a difficult task.
solution: The phrase "a difficult task" implies that driving in Lagos is extremely challenging. A "herculean task"  is a task that requires great strength and effort. It is synonymous with a difficult task.

options.a: It is an easy task to drive in Lagos
options.b: It is a difficult task driving Lagos
options.c: It is a herculean task driving in Lagos
options.d: It is not a difficult task to drive in Lagos

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

I am given a free hand to run this business.
solution: The phrase "given a free hand" means being granted the autonomy or freedom to make decisions and manage something without interference. Hence, option A correctly explains the conveyed meaning.

options.a: I am allowed to do my own business
options.d: I alone can choose my executives

question:
Select the option that best explains the information conveyed in the sentence. 

The government has promised to pay our gratuity, we need to have our fingers crossed.
solution: The phrase "have our fingers crossed" means to remain hopeful or wish for a positive outcome. Therefore, option C best explains the information conveyed in the sentence.

options.b: We should be united in purpose

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

She looks very pale.
solution: "Ruddy" refers to a healthy, red or flushed complexion, which is the opposite of "pale" (a lighter or less healthy complexion). Therefore, option A is the opposite in meaning to "pale."

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Before going to bed, make sure you extinguish all the lights.
solution: "Extinguish" means to turn off or put out. The opposite of turning off the lights (extinguishing) would be to "switch on" the lights. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "extinguish."

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Ebere's indolence contrasts with her sister's.
solution: "Indolence" refers to a state of laziness or avoidance of work. The opposite of laziness or indolence is "diligence," which means careful and persistent work or effort. Therefore, option C is the opposite in meaning to "indolence."

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The roads in major cities are broad.
solution: The word "broad" refers to something wide or spacious. The opposite of broad, in this context, is "narrow," which refers to something thin or restricted in width. Therefore, option D is the opposite in meaning to "broad."

question:
Choose the option opposite in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

He gave an indifferent look at his dismissal.
solution: "Indifferent" means showing a lack of interest or concern. The opposite of indifference, in this case, would be "concerned," meaning showing care or attention. Therefore, option B is the correct opposite in meaning.

question:
Choose the option that is nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The official has declared the election inconclusive.
solution: "Inconclusive" means something that does not provide clear results or answers. "Indeterminate" carries a similar meaning, indicating something that is uncertain or undefined, making it the closest in meaning.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

His reckless remark did not go unnoticed.
solution: A "reckless" remark refers to a statement made without careful thought or consideration and that is spontaneous or careless. "Unguarded" means something that is made without restraint or caution.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

The old fire station has obviously become derelict.
solution: "Derelict" means something that is in a state of disrepair or abandonment, which closely relates to "moribund," meaning something that is in a dying or decaying state. Both terms suggest neglect or decline.

question:
Choose the option nearest in meaning to the word or phrase underlined. 

Death is inevitable.
solution: "Inevitable" means something that cannot be avoided or prevented, which is synonymous with "unavoidable." Both terms suggest that an event or outcome is certain to happen.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I had to put up my house as _____ for the bank loan.
solution: When someone offers something of value, like a house, in exchange for a loan, it is referred to as "collateral." This is a common practice where the collateral serves as security for the loan in case the borrower fails to repay it.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The beggar would be grateful for even a _____ of bread.
solution: The phrase "even a crust of bread" is a common expression used to suggest that someone would be grateful for even a small, humble portion of bread, such as the crust, which is often considered the least desirable part.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

These properties were ____ sale before the fire gutted them.
solution: The phrase "up for sale" is commonly used to express that something is available or being offered for sale. In this case, it means the properties were available for sale before the fire destroyed them.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

The ______ of armed robbery has been on the increase.
solution: "Incidence" refers to the occurrence or frequency of an event, typically used in the context of events like crimes or diseases. Incidence on the other hand refers to a specific event or occurrence often unexpected or of a particular nature. In this sentence, we're discussing the general frequency or occurrence of armed robbery. This makes incidence the correct option in this context.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

She hurried off ______ and came back.
solution: "Fast" is the correct adverb form to describe the manner in which she hurried. "Fast" itself is both an adjective and an adverb. "Fastly" is not the standard adverb form.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Justina used to be the best-behaved girl, ____?
solution: In questions like this, the auxiliary verb used in the tag question should match the auxiliary verb or the verb used in the main sentence. All sentences have an auxiliary verb even though it is not visible. Sentences without visible auxiliary verbs carry the auxiliary verb "did" as in "She did used to be the best-behaved girl". Therefore, the correct tag question is "didn't she."

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

It was the _______ of brakes that drew our attention.
solution: The phrase "screeching of brakes" is a common expression used to describe the high-pitched, loud noise made by brakes when a vehicle is stopped suddenly.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Everyone knew the woman's _______ before his phone went off.
solution: The word "itinerary" refers to a planned route or journey that details the travel schedule or events. The other options are misspellings of the word.

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

Put Chioma's meal in the _______ pack.
solution:
A. shiny, new, red lunch

The other should be:

  1. Opinion or observation (shiny)
  2. Age or condition (new)
  3. Colour (red)
    Thus, "shiny, new, red" follows the correct order of adjectives before the noun "lunch."

question:
Choose the option that best completes the sentence. 

I was glad he called my attention ____ the matter.
solution: The correct preposition after the verb "call attention" is "to." We say "call someone's attention to something," meaning to make them aware of or focus on something.

question:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

The architect did the DESIGN.
solution: Emphatic stress is the placement of greater stress on a particular word in a sentence or utterance for emphasis, correction or clarification. To test for emphatic stress, the contrasting question should affirm or negate the specific word in capital letters ("DESIGN"). Option D contrasts "design" with "draft," making it the appropriate response to confirm emphasis.

options.b: Was the design done by the engineer?
options.c: Did the architect destroy the design?
options.d: Did the architect do the draft?

question:
The word in capital letters has the emphatic stress. Choose the options to which the given sentence relates. 

My only SON speaks like an orator.
solution: For emphatic stress on "SON," the contrasting question should challenge "son" by substituting it with a different noun (e.g., "daughter"). Option B correctly contrasts "son" with "daughter," confirming the emphasis.

options.a: Does my only son speak like an illiterate?
options.b: Does my only daughter speak like an orator?
options.c: Does my only son read like an orator?
options.d: Does your only son speak like an orator?

question: According to the passage, students perform poorly in examinations because
solution: The passage explicitly mentions that one of the main causes of mass failure in examinations is students' inability to imbibe a good reading culture. This is highlighted as the primary reason learners struggle with comprehension and examination performance.

options.b: they fail to identify the ethics of reading
options.c: they are too pre-occupied with social activities
options.d: of their inability to imbibe good reading culture

question: It is implied in the passage that
solution: The passage emphasises the importance of a good reading culture and its connection to effective comprehension. This implies that students who develop the habit of reading are more likely to grasp the writer's intentions and understand the text better.

options.a: students who cultivate reading habit easily comprehend the intention of the writer
options.b: the understanding of the writer will be strenuous for the students who prepare well enough
options.c: students who fail to imbibe a good reading culture will find it easy to know the intention of the writer
options.d: a student can easily understand the writer's intention whether he imbibes a good reading culture or not

question: From the passage, it is implied that
solution: The passage states, "most students see it as a tedious task that has to be performed in order to pass a particular examination." This implies that the majority of students perceive reading as a challenging activity.

options.a: few students see reading as a difficult task
options.b: all students see reading as a simple task
options.c: some students see reading as a difficult task
options.d: most students see reading as a difficult task

solution: A teacher is a "stakeholder" in the education system. This means that they have a vested interest in its success.

question: Fill the gap labelled 10
solution: "Teaching-learning process" refers to the interactive journey of teaching and learning.

question: Fill the gap labelled 11
solution: The phrase "move at the speed of the genius" expresses that teachers have to be adaptable to gifted students.

question: Fill the gap labelled 12

question: Fill the gap labelled 13
